title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Recovery Case: Rewrite Verification
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git rewrite verification increment in a history-rewrite plan and verification graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Rewrite Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

steps.title: Bound Rewrite Verification

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.

content.text: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Rewrite Verification while producing a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference. A defensible solution applies Rewrite Verification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-rewrite-verification — Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
git branch recovery/pre-rebase
git rebase --rebase-merges main
git range-diff recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
git diff recovery/pre-rebase^{tree} HEAD^{tree}
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Rewrite Verification evidence

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rewrite Verification into observable performance evidence.
